channel.title: 2026s on Today I Learned
channel.description: Recent content in 2026s on Today I Learned

item.description: That there’s a remove block that was added in Terraform 1.7.0 : https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/block/removed https://www.hashicorp.com/en/blog/terraform-1-7-adds-test-mocking-and-config-driven-remove#config-driven-remove

that pypi packages all have

  • RSS feeds of their release history
  • blake2b-256 hashes (but not blake3?)

That GitHub provides RSS feeds:

GitHub does offer built-in RSS feeds for certain activities, but they have key limitations:

  • Commits: Only available for a single branch (e.g., main). The URL format is https://github.com/{username}/{repo}/commits/{branch}.atom.
  • Releases, issues, and pull requests: These have dedicated RSS feeds (e.g., https://github.com/{username}/{repo}/releases.atom), but they don’t include commit details.

– https://www.codestudy.net/blog/setting-up-a-github-commit-rss-feed/#understanding-githubs-native-rss-limitations
That vim will jump to the previous cursor position if you hit Ctrl-O or ``.
That Mac has a built-in clipboard history feature: Cmd-Space Cmd-4, no copyq or flycut necessary.
That go tool buildid reads a build ID from go binaries: see https://alexanderobregon.substack.com/p/go-build-id-and-reproducibility It could be useful for caching in CI systems.

That there are two versions of the base32 encoding per RFC 4648: preferred base32 (A-Z 2-7), and base32hex (0-9 A-V =). See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Base32#Base_32_Encoding_with_Extended_Hex_Alphabet_per_%C2%A77
